name: Slider
description: An accessible range input that lets users pick a single value or a range from a continuous scale. Built on Base UI Slider with StyleX styling.

Root.description: Container that owns slider state (value, min, max, step, orientation) and provides it to child parts.

value.description: The controlled value. Pass an array for a multi-thumb (range) slider.

defaultValue.description: The uncontrolled initial value. Array form yields multiple thumbs.

onValueChange.description: Fires while the user is dragging or pressing keys.

onValueCommitted.description: Fires when the user releases the thumb or commits with the keyboard.

minStepsBetweenValues.description: Minimum step gap enforced between adjacent thumbs in a range slider.

format.description: Formatting applied to displayed values (Slider.Value, aria-valuetext).

dataAttributes.data-dragging: Present while a thumb is being dragged.

Value.description: Displays the current value(s) using the Root's locale + format.

children.description: Optional render function to fully customise the displayed value.

Control.description: The interactive surface that captures pointer events and hosts the track + thumbs.

dataAttributes.data-dragging: Present while a thumb is being dragged.

Track.description: The unfilled track behind the indicator.

size.description: Track thickness (sm=4px, md=8px, lg=12px) — matches Progress and Meter.

Indicator.description: The filled portion between min and the current value (or between two thumbs in a range slider).

Thumb.description: The draggable handle. Renders a nested input[type=range] with role=slider and aria-valuemin/max/now. Use multiple Thumbs (with index prop) for range sliders.

index.description: Required when rendering multiple thumbs for a range slider — corresponds to the index in the value array.

getAriaLabel.description: Returns aria-label for the underlying input — important for unlabelled thumbs.

getAriaValueText.description: Returns aria-valuetext — read aloud by screen readers in place of the raw number.

dataAttributes.data-dragging: Present while this thumb is being dragged.

intents.reasoning: Slider is the right control whenever the user is choosing a single number from a continuous range and rough position matters more than typing exact digits.
A multi-thumb Slider lets users pick a min/max bracket — the canonical range filter UI.
Vertical orientation is appropriate for fader-like controls (audio mixers, lighting).

avoidWhen.scenario: User needs to enter a precise number
avoidWhen.reasoning: Sliders trade precision for visual position. For exact numeric input use Number Field.
Slider is an input. For non-interactive value display use Progress (task progress) or Meter (scalar measurement).

avoidWhen.scenario: Choosing between a small set of discrete options
avoidWhen.reasoning: Sliders are for continuous ranges. For 2–7 discrete choices a Radio group or Toggle Group is clearer.
A two-state slider is unfamiliar. Use Switch for boolean toggles.

examples.name: controlled
examples.code:
const [value, setValue] = useState(40);
<Slider.Root value={value} onValueChange={(v) => setValue(v as number)}>
  <Slider.Label>Brightness</Slider.Label>
  <Slider.Value />
  <Slider.Control>
    <Slider.Track>
      <Slider.Indicator />
      <Slider.Thumb />
    </Slider.Track>
  </Slider.Control>
</Slider.Root>
